title: Machine-readable requirement contract (companion to docs/requirement.md)
description: Produced by product_manager in phase 1. Lists every requirement as a structured row so downstream phases can iterate without parsing markdown.

quantitative_constraints.description: Machine-readable register for every numeric/quantified rule found in docs/requirement.md (e.g. 'at least N', 'no more than M ms', 'coverage >= K%'). Downstream phases (architecture, implementation, verification) iterate this list to ensure each constraint is satisfied. Empty array is allowed only when the requirement document contains zero quantified rules.

standard_formulas.description: Domain algorithms / industry-standard formulas the requirement names. Captured here in pseudocode + worked examples so the architect locks them in behavioral_contract.domain_invariants[] and the developer must implement them bit-for-bit. Empty array is allowed when the requirement does not invoke any domain standard.

quantitative_constraint.description: A single quantified rule extracted from a requirement. Language-agnostic: 'target' uses an abstract artifact descriptor (e.g. 'playable_units', 'persisted_record_kinds'), 'operator' is one of a fixed set, 'verifiable_via' is a reproducible expression downstream phases must satisfy.

target.description: Abstract artifact descriptor (free-form short identifier). Architect maps this to concrete fields in stack_contract / behavioral_contract.

unit.description: Free-form unit label (e.g. 'level', 'ms', 'percent', 'mb'); kept as text for human readability.

verifiable_via.description: Reproducible expression naming the downstream artifact / count / value that satisfies this constraint. Examples: 'count(<glob>)', 'sum(<dotted.path>)', 'len(stack_contract.subsystems[name=X].components)'. Architect uses this to bind the constraint to a concrete measurable artifact.

standard_formula.description: A domain-standard formula the requirement names. 'formula' is single-line language-agnostic pseudocode using named operators (max/min/sum/round/clamp/abs). At least 2 worked examples are required, including at least one edge case (boundary / empty / overflow / clamp).

rationale.description: Why this exact form (and not a softer/harder variant). Critical for cases where domain semantics depend on edge handling.
